preferred_translation: delight and ease born of mental composure
definition: A jhana formula expression for the delight and ease born of mental composure in the second mental theme.
notes: Use this record for the delight-and-ease phrase attached to the second mental theme. Keep the line coordinated with pīti and sukha as distinct but paired factors and with samadhi as mental composure rather than concentration. Because live translation surfaces already read the phrase through the governed samadhi policy, this minor entry keeps mental composure explicit rather than leaving samadhi untranslated in English.

example_phrases.translation: delight and ease born of mental composure

translation_policy.default_scope: Use in second-jhana formula contexts for the delight-and-ease pair born of mental composure.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not collapse pīti and sukha into one vague feeling-word or reframe samadhi as concentration. Use the Pali only where the formula itself is under technical discussion.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the phrase from flattening the paired factors or from importing concentration language back into the jhana sequence.

literal_meaning: delight and ease born of samadhi
